data.narrative:
Terrestrial Energy is a public-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $280M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 identifiable peers: 3 trading publicly (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. This represents a nascent and heterogeneous peer universe typical of frontier technology sectors where stage, technology pathway, and commercialization timeline vary significantly across participants.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy peer set lacks sufficient homogeneity at comparable stages and similar valuation tiers to support reliable triangulation. Only two same-stage peers were identified, and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier comps exist. The standard threshold for peer-median analysis—a minimum of three datapoints meeting narrow filters—was not met. Relying on all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the wide dispersion in technology maturity and capital intensity across the 11-firm universe.

Without applicable triangulation methods supported by the available data, a quantitative valuation range cannot be defensibly derived. Future valuation work would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set through additional market discovery; (2) application of discounted cash flow or sum-of-the-parts methodology, conditional on management projections not provided here; or (3) staged reassessment as the sector matures and peer comparability improves. The primary valuation uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient narrow-filter peer anchors.
